title: STANDARD FOR EXTRA HARD GRATING CHEESE CXS 278-1978

sections.section: STANDARD FOR EXTRA HARD GRATING CHEESE CXS 278-1978
sections.summary: Title/heading: Standard for Extra Hard Grating Cheese (CXS 278-1978); not a substantive provision.

sections.section: Formerly CODEX STAN C-35-1978. Adopted in 1978. Amended in 2018, 2021, 2022.
sections.summary: Provides the standard's history: formerly CODEX STAN C-35-1978, adopted 1978 and amended in 2018, 2021 and 2022.
Designates the product name as 'Extra Hard Grating' cheese.
Indicates the depositing country for the standard as the United States of America.

Specifies allowed raw materials: cow, goat or sheep milk (and mixtures) and authorized additions—necessary (harmless lactic starter cultures, rennet/other coagulating enzymes, sodium chloride) and optional (harmless flavour‑producing bacteria and harmless enzymes for flavour development, with preparation solids not to exceed 0.1% of the milk weight).

sections.section: 3.1 Kind of milk: Cow's milk, goat's milk or sheep's milk and mixtures of these milks.
sections.summary: States permitted milk types for the cheese: cow's, goat's or sheep's milk and mixtures thereof.

Details authorized additions: necessary additions (harmless lactic starter cultures, rennet or suitable coagulating enzymes, sodium chloride) and optional harmless flavour‑producing bacteria and enzymes (enzyme preparation solids ≤0.1% of milk weight).
Non‑substantive heading listing that the section covers various dimensions and weights (no specific measurements provided).
Describes rind characteristics for the cheese where present: consistency extra hard; appearance dry and may be coated with vegetable oil, food‑grade wax or plastic materials; colour amber.
Specifies that the rind consistency (when present) is extra hard.

sections.section: 5.4.2 Appearance: Dry, may be coated with vegetable oil, food grade wax or plastic materials.
sections.summary: Specifies rind appearance as dry and allows optional coatings of vegetable oil, food‑grade wax or plastic materials.
Specifies the rind colour (when present) as amber.
Describes the cheese body: texture granular and slightly brittle; colour ranging from natural uncoloured to light cream.
Specifies the cheese body texture as granular and slightly brittle.

sections.section: 5.5.2 Colour: Natural uncoloured to light cream colour.
sections.summary: Specifies the cheese body colour as natural uncoloured to light cream.

sections.section: 5.6 Holes (when holes are a typical characteristic of the variety)
sections.summary: When holes are typical for the variety, specifies their characteristics: few in number, small round shape, approximately 1–2 mm in size, and appearing as characteristic gas holes.
Specifies that the number of holes (when typical) is few.
Specifies that the shape of holes (when typical) is small and round.
Specifies the typical hole size as approximately 1–2 mm.
Describes the holes' appearance as characteristic gas holes.

sections.section: 5.7 Minimum fat content in dry matter: 32%
sections.summary: Sets a compositional requirement for the cheese: minimum fat in dry matter of 32%.
Sets a compositional limit for the cheese: maximum moisture content of 36%.

Outlines the cheese manufacturing procedure: coagulation by rennet or suitable enzymes (optionally with lactic starter); milk may be raw or pasteurized (if pasteurized, heated to ≥72 °C for 15 s); fermentation by lactic or other flavour‑producing cultures; and maturation involving salting options and ageing in a cool, well‑aerated or temperature‑controlled room for not less than 6 months.

sections.section: 6.1 Method of coagulation: Rennet or other suitable coagulating enzymes; with the possible addition of a lactic acid starter.
sections.summary: Requires coagulation using rennet or other suitable coagulating enzymes, with optional addition of a lactic acid starter.

sections.section: 6.2 Heat treatment: Milk may be raw or pasteurized. If pasteurized the milk is heated to not less than 72 °C (161 °F) for 15 seconds.
sections.summary: Allows milk to be raw or pasteurized and, if pasteurized, mandates heating to not less than 72 °C (161 °F) for 15 seconds.

sections.section: 6.3 Fermentation procedure: Lactic acid fermentation or other flavour producing cultures and enzymes.
sections.summary: Specifies fermentation by lactic acid fermentation or other flavour‑producing cultures and enzymes.

sections.section: 6.4 Maturation procedure: After  the  curd  which  may  be  lightly  salted  is  shaped  into  forms,  the  cheese  may again be salted in brine, dry salted or both; held in a cool and well aerated or temperature controlled room for not less than 6 months.
sections.summary: Describes maturation: after shaping (and possible light salting), the cheese may be brine‑salted, dry‑salted or both, and must be held in a cool, well‑aerated or temperature‑controlled room for at least 6 months.

For checking compliance with this Standard, use the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) that are relevant to the Standard's provisions.

Labelling requirements: only cheese conforming to this Standard may be called 'Extra Hard Grating Cheese' or a recognized variety (coined/fanciful names allowed if not misleading and accompanied by 'Extra Hard Grating Cheese'); labelling must follow the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978); labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.section: 8.1 Only  cheese  conforming  with  this  Standard  may  be  designated  Extra  Hard  Grating  Cheese  or  any recognized variety name in the consuming country. A 'coined' or 'fanciful' name, may be used however, provided it is not misleading and is accompanied by the phrase 'Extra Hard Grating Cheese'.
sections.summary: Only cheese that conforms to this Standard may be designated 'Extra Hard Grating Cheese' or a recognized variety name in the consuming country, although a coined or fanciful name may be used if not misleading and accompanied by the phrase 'Extra Hard Grating Cheese'.

sections.section: 8.2 It shall be labelled in conformity with the appropriate sections of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 2831978).
sections.summary: The product shall be labelled in conformity with the appropriate sections of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978).
Section heading only: 'Labelling of non-retail containers' (no substantive provisions in this entry).

sections.section: 8.4 The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
sections.summary: Labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
